intake_log.raw_input: Her Reset Button: 60 Minutes to Yourself a workshop for the mom who's hitting the decision fatigue wall and just needs a break. I'll hold your brain and you leave with (at least) one less decision to make.

Her Reset Button: Income After AI working in tech is like riding a roller coaster through the wild west today. Jobs come as fast as they go and as long as you rely on someone else to pay the bills, it's the same old rollercoaster. Take a 60 minute break from the ups and downs and leave with a straight path to what people mean when they say, "I got rich with AI."

content.title: Her Reset Button: Income After AI
content.description: working in tech is like riding a roller coaster through the wild west today. Jobs come as fast as they go and as long as you rely on someone else to pay the bills, it's the same old rollercoaster. Take a 60 minute break from the ups and downs and leave with a straight path to what people mean when they say, "I got rich with AI."
content.transcript:
What If the Thing You're Worried About Is Actually the Thing That Sets You Free?
​Sounds familiar, right? The obstacle is the way.

​Listen.
​You've spent years solving problems, managing people, navigating change, and figuring things out when there wasn't a clear path forward.

​Often while someone else stood in front of the room taking the credit, collecting the praise, and calling it leadership.

​Leave that behind. Let that Sh*t GO!

​Instead, let's look forward to what you do well.

​Because your skills, your values, your input matters.

​Maybe more than ever.

​While everyone else is counting the jobs being eliminated, I'm paying attention to the businesses being born.
​New industries are being born as you read this

​New ways for one person to create value

​New methods for solving problems

​New streams of revenue

​And it's all available like low-hanging fruit for the woman who braves biting the apple.

​Think about the people who bought Apple stock when most people thought it was a hobby computer company.

​Most of us don't get many chances to witness the beginning of something this significant.

​This is one of them.

​The Good News
​You already have what you need.

​Work ethic

​Adaptability

​Communication skills

​Resilience

​The ability to care about people while solving real problems

​The future won't belong to the people with the most credentials.

​It belongs to the people who can:

​Identify a problem

​Find an answer

​Adapt to changing conditions

​Communicate that answer in a way that creates clarity for someone else

​Skills Worth Strengthening
​Finding what matters

​Connecting dots

​Translating complexity into understanding

​Valuing the process of trying new tools to create value for yourself and others

​Those are the skills you want to strengthen because your babies are watching you. They will model YOU.

​Because You Chose to Be Brave at a Time When Most Stayed Scared
​You get to choose:

​The industry (or industries)

​Your interests

​The problems you want to solve

​Doing the best parts of work you enjoy

​And increasingly, technology helps carry the parts you don't.

​Not so you can work more.

​But so you can:

​Play with your kids more

​Call the friends you're missing

​Do more of what makes you feel like YOU

​Build something that's adaptable and belongs to you

​Create a future that isn't dependent on someone else's roadmap

​Your Children Are Watching
​Whether we like it or not, our children are watching how we respond to change.

​Their future depends on parents who are willing to:

​Adapt

​Stay resilient

​Lead

​Not from fear. Let fear push you.

​And let possibility pull you.

​What You'll Leave This Workshop With
​A somatic reset for your body and nervous system so you can think clearly instead of react quickly

​Two prompts to help manage your kids' latest behavioral challenge and boost your Momfidence

​A custom AI Agent that helps you think through future decisions, contrarily, clearly, & quickly

​A fresh perspective on a scary topic

​One less choice to make because the answer becomes clear. YOU are the answer.

​It's time that you take ownership for yourself, become the lead on your family's future, and be the mom your kids are proud of.
​What This Isn't
​It's not a tech class.

​It's not a sales presentation.

​It's not another productivity system.

​What This Is
​One hour with a woman who wouldn't take no for an answer. She's a best friend, a sister, a mother, a daughter. She is a reflection of you and she's holding the ground to help you stop spinning, start thinking clearly, and take your next step toward a future that is more secure than your future has ever been.

​You remember what they said.

​"The future is feminine."
​Well, the future is here now.

​You might as well meet it on purpose.

​Get your $10 pass, your 60 minute class, and let's begin acting like the generation that has every lifetime worth of wisdom available to her now.

content.title: Her Reset Button: 60 Minutes to Yourself
content.description: a workshop for the mom who's hitting the decision fatigue wall and just needs a break. I'll hold your brain and you leave with (at least) one less decision to make.
content.transcript:
Have you tried unplugging and plugging yourself back in?

​You’ve made a thousand choices before eating leftover dinner for lunch on Monday.

​What to buy. Which model? How much?

​What to cook. Pizza again? Kale tomorrow.

​What to schedule. Hair can wait. Toes are a must. Summer camp next week.

​What to ignore. Thank God for the new 'call screen' feature. Does it have to show how many?

​What to worry about. Traffic? Friends? The check engine light.

​What to learn. With what time? It's not 2020 anymore Todo.

​What to do next. Date night? Yeah, right!? Garden? More like Gardyn. Probably, feed the cat.

​On top of having kids, every app, reel, feature, podcast, “life changing shot,” and your husband asks you to make:

​One. More. Choice.

​Forgive me now.

​But I promise this isn’t a class.

​It doesn’t lead to a course.

​There’s no hype cycle.

​No sales presentation.

​No Q&A.

​Just you, your laptop, a notebook + tea, and 60 minutes of guided time to yourself.

​A chance to think a little less so you can accomplish a little more with what you already got.

​Like AI.

​Capable. Imperfect.

​Done.

​Just come breathe.

​Leave with a quieter mind, a little more patience, and one less to-do in your head.

​What You'll Leave With

​The Audit
Back to your factory settings. No more obligations for the sake of "should." You'll leave with a clear picture of what you actually need today, this week, and this season of life.

​The Somatic Reset
A guided practice to move from your "Not Another Choice" eye-roll to your "F*ck Yes" frequency. A reset for your body, mind, and nervous system you can return to whenever life feels too loud.

​The Missing Instructions
Two simple prompts to better understand your child's unique behaviors and support them with newfound Momfidence.

​Your Custom Agent
The beginning of a brain outside your head. Part thinking partner, part decision assistant, all relief. A personalized AI guide that helps you clear mental clutter, organize priorities, and make decisions with ease.

​The Details:

​When: Saturday, 8/1 at 12 PM

​Where: Virtual (GoogleMeet link provided upon registration)

​Investment: $10 (Beta price for this inaugural vibe check)

​Come as you are: exhausted, skeptical, or with your hands in the air. I’ll meet you there.

content.title: Carousel: 7 AI questions every mom can ask this week (without becoming 'an AI person')
content.description: Save-magnet carousel. 7 slides, one question per slide, each one practical and non-intimidating. Ends with comment trigger.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — high save potential

content.title: Why I failed before (passwords, crashes, error codes) — and how AI/agents reduce that fragility
content.description: Podcast episode. Personal story about tech failure and overwhelm, pivot to how AI removes single points of failure in your life and business.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — strong anchor story material

content.title: Analogy reel: ChatGPT is Google Maps for your brain
content.description: 15-second reel. The analogy does all the work. Nobody feared Google Maps — they should feel the same about ChatGPT.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — viral potential, very shareable

content.title: Homeschooling without formal education — using AI to sanity-check lessons
content.description: Podcast episode. How Mandi uses AI to verify homeschool content, fill gaps, and feel confident teaching without a teaching degree.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — strong niche crossover (AI moms + homeschool)

content.title: Giving as strategy: what if we gave our house away?
content.description: Podcast episode. Vision and values content. Explores generosity as a business and life strategy, not just charity.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — deeply personal, vision-level content

content.title: AI and humanity (for AI haters): what's left of being human?
content.description: Podcast episode. Direct address to the skeptics. Warm, non-defensive, genuinely curious exploration of what AI can't replace.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — great for reaching non-believers

content.title: Parenting fears vs the world messing up your kids — where AI helps vs hurts
content.description: Podcast episode. Honest two-sided conversation. Where Mandi trusts AI with parenting and where she draws the line.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — emotionally resonant, shareable

content.title: Who tried to make a birthday: [finish this story]
content.description: Incomplete idea — a personal story about someone attempting to make a birthday happen. Needs more context from Mandi to develop.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — story got cut off, needs finishing

content.title: Wash or not to wash — the question every mom asks before stepping into the shower
content.description: Comedy reel. The universal mom dilemma. Relatable hook, no explanation needed. Could be voiceover over B-roll or talking head.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — comedy gold, high share potential

content.title: 'Write me a plan to never run out of diapers' → reality: the plan fails immediately
content.description: Comedy reel showing AI giving a perfect diaper plan followed by real mom chaos. Proves AI is useful AND funny when it meets real life.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — perfect AI Mom brand moment

content.title: The amount of coffee + ibuprofen I subsist on (and why moms are running on patches, not fuel)
content.description: Relatable reel or carousel. Opens with confession, pivots to how AI reduces the mental load that's draining moms in the first place.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — high saves, 'send this to every mom' content

content.title: If an AI ran my diaper bag: alerts at 3 diapers. If I run it: alerts at 0 diapers, in public.
content.description: PRIORITY. Comedy reel. The contrast IS the hook. Zero setup needed. Under 15 seconds. Most viral idea in the batch.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — POST THIS FIRST. Highest viral potential in the batch.

content.title: Do I get up and make coffee or masturbate?
content.description: PRIORITY. Comedy reel. The most scroll-stopping idea in the batch. Bold, honest, funny, 100% Mandi voice. Goes viral or gets banned — either way people talk.
content.notes: From Notion idea inbox — POST THIS SECOND. Most disruptive hook in the batch.
